proportionality.description: AI Act uses a risk-based classification: prohibited, high-risk, limited risk, minimal risk

tiers.name: prohibited
tiers.applies_to: AI systems per Art. 5 (social scoring, real-time biometric ID in public spaces with exceptions, etc.)

tiers.name: high_risk
tiers.applies_to: AI systems in Annex III areas or safety components of Annex I products (Art. 6)
tiers.note: Full conformity assessment, CE marking, post-market monitoring. Applies from 2026-08-02.

tiers.name: limited_risk
tiers.applies_to: AI systems interacting with persons, emotion recognition, biometric categorization, deepfakes (Art. 50)

tiers.name: gpai
tiers.note: Transparency, documentation, copyright compliance. Systemic risk models have additional obligations. Applies from 2025-08-02.

pitfalls.description: AI Act applies in phases: prohibitions (Feb 2025), GPAI (Aug 2025), high-risk Annex III (Aug 2026), high-risk Annex I (Aug 2027). Assess which deadlines apply to the specific AI system.

Art. 6(2) high-risk classification applies to Annex III systems ONLY if they pose a significant risk. Art. 6(3) allows providers to self-assess and declare non-high-risk if criteria are met, but must document the assessment.

GPAI models with systemic risk (>10^25 FLOPs training compute or Commission designation) face additional obligations: adversarial testing, incident tracking, cybersecurity protections. Threshold is in Art. 51(2).

cross_regulation.note: AI Act requirements stack with GDPR automated decision-making provisions (Art. 22) and data protection impact assessments (Art. 35).
Revised Product Liability Directive covers AI-caused damage. AI Act conformity evidence may be relevant to PLD claims.

national_implementation.pattern: AI Act is a regulation (directly applicable). Search Law MCPs for national implementing measures (e.g., national AI supervisory authority designation).

timeline.note: Full high-risk requirements for Annex III AI systems

citation_format: Cite as: AI Act Art. X(Y). For Annex references: AI Act Annex N, Section X.
